channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: China Develops Large Composite Module for Long March 10 Reusable Rocket
item.description: China has unveiled its first reusable 5-meter-wide composite propulsion module, marking a significant advancement in its aerospace capabilities. Developed by the China Aerospace and Technology Corpora...

item.title: Unauthorized Access Reported on Anthropic's Enterprise Cybersecurity Tool Mythos
item.description: Anthropic's recently announced cybersecurity tool, Mythos, designed for enterprise security, may have been accessed by an unauthorized group. According to Bloomberg, members of a private online forum ...

item.title: US Extends Ceasefire with Iran on Pakistan's Mediation Until Tehran Presents Unified Proposal
item.description: In a significant development, US President Donald Trump has announced the extension of the ceasefire between the United States and Iran, following a request from Pakistan’s Chief of Defence Staff Asim...

item.title: Mathematicians Make New Progress on the Longstanding Lonely Runner Conjecture
item.description: The “lonely runner” problem is a deceptively simple mathematical puzzle that has challenged researchers for decades. It imagines several runners moving around a circular track at different constant sp...

item.title: Nigeria Investigates Alleged Coup Attempt, Pope Addresses Global Concerns, and Tanzanian Athletes Excel
item.description: Recent headlines from Tanzania and beyond highlight a range of critical events. Nigeria is currently investigating six individuals accused of plotting a coup to overthrow President Tinubu, adding to t...

item.title: UK launches mRNA vaccine trial targeting H5N1 bird flu pandemic risk
item.description: The United Kingdom has begun a large-scale clinical trial of a new mRNA vaccine designed to protect against the H5N1 strain of bird flu, which scientists consider a potential pandemic threat. The tria...

item.title: Study Links Loneliness to Lower Baseline Memory but Not Faster Cognitive Decline in Older Adults
item.description: A large longitudinal study of more than 10,000 adults aged 65 to 94 across 12 European countries provides new insight into how loneliness relates to memory in later life. Using data from the SHARE pro...

item.title: US Lawmaker Accuses Nigerian Defence Minister of Attempted Bribery Over Security Report
item.description: Na matter wey dey cause plenty gist now be say US lawmaker Kimberly Daniels don accuse Nigeria's Minister of State for Defence, Bello Matawalle, say e try bribe her make she change report wey dey talk...

item.title: Desert City: Transforming an Industrial Site into Europe’s Largest Cactus Garden
item.description: On the outskirts of Madrid, a striking transformation has taken place where an industrial wasteland has been turned into Desert City, Europe's largest cactus garden. Designed by the Madrid-based studi...

item.title: Two UK winners share major EuroMillions jackpot prize

item.title: Parents Advocate for Stronger Online Safety Laws for Children in Washington
item.description: A group of parents and child safety advocates gathered on Capitol Hill on April 21, 2026, to push for federal legislation aimed at improving online safety for minors. About 60 parents, many of whom ha...

item.title: Ensuring stable financing is key for successful Black industrial participation in South Africa
item.description: South Africa’s industrial policy discussions often focus on targets such as ownership thresholds, localisation quotas and transformation metrics, which represent the 'ceiling' of policy ambitions. How...

item.title: UK PM Keir Starmer Faces Controversy Over Mandelson's US Envoy Appointment Amid Scandal
item.description: Downing Street has rejected claims that UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s office applied pressure on civil servants to rush the approval of Peter Mandelson as the UK envoy to the United States. The con...

item.title: Kano Film Censorship Board Closes Herbal Shop Over Indecent Advertisement Involving Children
item.description: The Kano State Film Censorship Board don shut down one herbal medicine shop for the state after plenty people complain about the way the owner dey use small children to advertise sexual enhancement pr...

item.title: President Tinubu Replaces Finance Minister, Appoints Oyedele
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has made a dramatic change at the top of Nigeria’s economic leadership, relieving Wale Edun of his position as Minister of Finance and replacing him with Taiwo Oyedele. The...

item.title: Man pleads guilty to racially motivated sexual assault on Sikh woman
item.description: A man has admitted to committing a sexual assault against a Sikh woman, in an attack that was aggravated by racial motives. The victim, whose identity has been protected, experienced a traumatic and v...

item.title: US Lawmakers Express Concerns Over South Korea's Trade Practices Favoring China
item.description: Over 50 House members, led by Representative Darrell Issa, have expressed deep concern over South Korea's trade policies, alleging discriminatory practices targeting American companies while seemingly...

item.title: SpaceX Achieves 600 Falcon 9 First Stage Landings and Retires JRTI Drone Ship
item.description: On April 19, 2026, SpaceX successfully launched a Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg's SLC-4E, carrying 25 Starlink v2 satellites in mission Starlink Group 17-22. The booster, B1097, completed its eighth...

item.title: Ofcom probes Telegram over claims of child sexual abuse material sharing on messaging platform
item.description: Ofcom, the UK communications regulator, has opened an investigation into the popular messaging app Telegram over concerns that child sexual abuse material (CSAM) may be being shared on the platform. T...

item.title: Karachi Red Line BRT Lot-2 contract cancelled after delays and performance issues
item.description: Authorities have officially terminated the contract for Lot 2 of Karachi’s Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) Red Line project, citing continuous delays and poor performance by the contractor. This section of th...
